title: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion
display_title: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by LonelyAsuka » Mon Feb 03, 2025 10:51 pm hi, this is my first post in this forum and I'm glad to be apart of this community. I'm trying to get my friend into Evangelion (the original series) and when I asked him about it, he told me that a friend of his told him about a certain SA scene, referring to the hospital scene, and he refuses to watch the show because of it. He dropped Berserk completely because of it and hates authors of any media depicting such scenes, even when used as a plot device etc. To my understanding he doesn't like anime unless it's from the 80's to 90's era, which is odd because he loves edgerunners (i understand ER isn'ttechnically an anime but i thought it was worth mentioning) and when I told him the show's older he still didn't care. I even mentioned to him that the scene isn't in the show, it's in the film following the ending of the show and despite this, he still doesn't want to watch even the show. The only reason I reccomended him the show was 1 because he likes thought-provoking and emotional shows, and 2 because I need more people to discuss the show with, however he won't bite. I haven't pressed him on the matter since, it was 2 discussions spanning from once last year and once a few days ago. Anyways sorry for the long post, I'm sure there have been many similar threads made in the past and for that I'm sorry, anything helps thank you.

[]: Re: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by Vqs88_C3H6O » Thu Feb 06, 2025 5:45 am It's very hard. But if given the opportunity, could you try watching it with him? when friends watch a certain work together, even if he doesn't like it, he usually won't leave directly. Maybe after watching a few episodes, he will fall in love with this work.

[]: Re: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Feb 24, 2025 11:22 pm I’m not sure if you're still trying to convince him, but the best approach is to simply show him the first episode. Avoid jumping ahead to things like EOE, Asuka, Lilith, etc., as that can be overwhelming and confusing. Just start with the first few episodes. I've gotten a lot of friends into Evangelion using this method. It doesn’t always work (some people feel the first episodes are slow), but more often than not, they’re hooked by how well it’s done and end up continuing on their own.

[]: Re: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by CSPAN » Sat Mar 01, 2025 6:23 pm I recently did a watch-along with a friend of mine that isn't particularly interested in anime overall, but had heard enough good things about Eva to give it a shot. Basically a critical mass of our mutual friend group had recommended it. He had a hard time with the first several episodes before being drawn in around e16, but was extremely unimpressed with the ending and it took a bit of prodding for a few days to get him to sit down and watch EoE, which he did generally enjoy. In the interest of not being pushy and overbearing, I have only casually mentioned the Rebuilds. I will probably wait a while before asking him when he's going to watch them.

[]: Re: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 02, 2025 4:40 pm if that's a hard boundary for him & it really triggers him that much, it might be best to just respect his choice. If he dropped Berserk , clearly good quality isn't going to make up for his discomfort with this type of material; Ppl just vary in their sensitivity to things.

[]: Re: How do I convince my friend to watch Evangelion?             Quote Post by Rumus » Mon Mar 17, 2025 6:47 am I think the best way is to just tell them to give the first episode a try. If they don't like the first two, then don't force them into it (however you could tease them that they missed out on epic stuff, if they won't get mad from lighthearted teasing). If they like it, their mental health is in for a ride.
